name: HW3 Applied: Zero-Shot Super Resolution

tests.name: model_check
tests.description: ZSSRNet is defined, is nn.Module, and produces correct output shape
tests.code:
model = ZSSRNet()
assert isinstance(model, nn.Module), 'ZSSRNet must be an nn.Module'
print(f'ZSSRNet params: {sum(p.numel() for p in model.parameters()):,}')

# Test output shape with scale_factor
x = torch.randn(1, 3, 16, 16)
y = model(x, scale_factor=4)
if y.dim() == 3: y = y.unsqueeze(0)
assert y.shape[-2] == 64 and y.shape[-1] == 64, f'Expected (..., 64, 64), got {y.shape}'
assert y.shape[-3] == 3, f'Expected 3 output channels, got {y.shape[-3]}'

# Test with target_size
y2 = model(x, scale_factor=4, target_size=(60, 60))
if y2.dim() == 3: y2 = y2.unsqueeze(0)
assert y2.shape[-2] == 60 and y2.shape[-1] == 60, f'Expected (..., 60, 60), got {y2.shape}'

# Test with 3D input
x3d = torch.randn(3, 16, 16)
y3d = model(x3d, scale_factor=4)
assert y3d.dim() == 3, f'3D input should give 3D output, got {y3d.dim()}D'
assert y3d.shape == (3, 64, 64), f'Expected (3, 64, 64), got {y3d.shape}'

print('model_check: PASSED')

tests.name: sr_image_1
tests.description: ZSSR on even-sized test image (400x400) with continuous PSNR scoring
tests.code:
_sf = 4
_t0 = _time.time()

# Downscale HR to get LR
_lr1 = resize_bicubic(_img1_hr, scale_factor=1.0/_sf)
print(f'Image 1: HR={_img1_hr.shape}, LR={_lr1.shape}')

# Run student's pipeline
_sr1 = train_and_evaluate(_lr1, scale_factor=_sf, device='cpu')
assert _sr1 is not None, 'train_and_evaluate returned None'
if isinstance(_sr1, np.ndarray): _sr1 = torch.from_numpy(_sr1).float()
if _sr1.dim() == 4: _sr1 = _sr1.squeeze(0)
assert _sr1.dim() == 3, f'Expected 3D output (C,H,W), got {_sr1.dim()}D'
assert _sr1.shape[0] == 3, f'Expected 3 channels, got {_sr1.shape[0]}'

# Resize to match HR if needed
_th, _tw = _img1_hr.shape[-2], _img1_hr.shape[-1]
if _sr1.shape[-2] != _th or _sr1.shape[-1] != _tw:
    _sr1 = resize_bicubic(_sr1, size=(_th, _tw))
_sr1 = torch.clamp(_sr1, 0, 1)

# Compute PSNR
_bic1 = torch.clamp(resize_bicubic(_lr1, size=(_th, _tw)), 0, 1)
_psnr_bic1 = psnr(_bic1, _img1_hr)
_psnr_sr1 = psnr(_sr1, _img1_hr)
_p1 = max(0, _psnr_sr1 - _psnr_bic1)

print(f'Bicubic PSNR: {_psnr_bic1:.2f} dB')
print(f'ZSSR PSNR:    {_psnr_sr1:.2f} dB')
print(f'Improvement:  {_p1:+.2f} dB')
print(f'Time: {_time.time()-_t0:.1f}s')

# Score: 20 base (runs) + 10 (beats bicubic) + up to 15 (PSNR bonus)
_score1 = 20
if _psnr_sr1 > _psnr_bic1:
    _score1 += 10
    _score1 += min(15, round(_p1 * 8))
_score1 = min(45, _score1)
print(f'Score: {_score1}/45')
_results['tests']['sr_image_1'] = f'BONUS: {_score1}'

tests.name: sr_image_2
tests.description: ZSSR on odd-sized test image (300x451) with continuous PSNR scoring
tests.code:
_sf = 4
_t0 = _time.time()

_lr2 = resize_bicubic(_img2_hr, scale_factor=1.0/_sf)
print(f'Image 2: HR={_img2_hr.shape}, LR={_lr2.shape}')

try:
    _sr2 = train_and_evaluate(_lr2, scale_factor=_sf, device='cpu')
    assert _sr2 is not None, 'train_and_evaluate returned None'
    if isinstance(_sr2, np.ndarray): _sr2 = torch.from_numpy(_sr2).float()
    if _sr2.dim() == 4: _sr2 = _sr2.squeeze(0)
    assert _sr2.dim() == 3, f'Expected 3D output (C,H,W), got {_sr2.dim()}D'
    assert _sr2.shape[0] == 3, f'Expected 3 channels, got {_sr2.shape[0]}'

    _th2, _tw2 = _img2_hr.shape[-2], _img2_hr.shape[-1]
    if _sr2.shape[-2] != _th2 or _sr2.shape[-1] != _tw2:
        _sr2 = resize_bicubic(_sr2, size=(_th2, _tw2))
    _sr2 = torch.clamp(_sr2, 0, 1)

    _bic2 = torch.clamp(resize_bicubic(_lr2, size=(_th2, _tw2)), 0, 1)
    _psnr_bic2 = psnr(_bic2, _img2_hr)
    _psnr_sr2 = psnr(_sr2, _img2_hr)
    _p2 = max(0, _psnr_sr2 - _psnr_bic2)

    print(f'Bicubic PSNR: {_psnr_bic2:.2f} dB')
    print(f'ZSSR PSNR:    {_psnr_sr2:.2f} dB')
    print(f'Improvement:  {_p2:+.2f} dB')
    print(f'Time: {_time.time()-_t0:.1f}s')

    _score2 = 20
    if _psnr_sr2 > _psnr_bic2:
        _score2 += 10
        _score2 += min(15, round(_p2 * 8))
    _score2 = min(45, _score2)
    print(f'Score: {_score2}/45')
    _results['tests']['sr_image_2'] = f'BONUS: {_score2}'
except Exception as _e2:
    print(f'Image 2 failed: {_e2}')
    _results['tests']['sr_image_2'] = f'FAIL: {_e2}'

tests.name: psnr_excellence
tests.description: Excellence bonus based on average PSNR improvement across both images
